checks.a_analysis: The provided text in the converted output for the present sections (NC 911 Board Monthly Dispatch, NENA 911 Goes To Washington, Eastern Region Report, Brett Renfrow retirement, and 'Want to Share') accurately reflects the text in the source PDF. There are no hallucinated or invented paragraphs in the body of the document. The metadata marker regarding omitted sections is a technical pipeline note and not a fabrication of source content.

The sections containing contact information (such as the NMAC contact info on page 15, the Statescoop voting link on page 11, and the Finance email addresses on page 21) are missing from the output due to the truncated conversion. No contact information was fabricated in the provided segments.

The pipeline output explicitly omits a massive portion of the document. Pages 6 through 22 are entirely absent from the conversion result. This includes critical sections such as the North Central, South Central, and Western Region Reports, the PSAP Assessment Coordinator Report, the Word from Your RCs, the entire 'Nerd Herd' technical section (4 pages), the 'Education Station' training section (3 pages), Finance Team updates and reminders, and the first PSAP Highlight (Kevin Anderson).
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains content on pages 6-22, including the North Central Region Report (p. 6), South Central Region Report (p. 7), Western Region Report (p. 8-9), PSAP Assessment Coordinator Report (p. 10), RC Word (p. 11), Nerd Herd (p. 12-15), Education Station (p. 16-18), Finance Team (p. 19-21), and PSAP Highlights - Kevin Anderson (p. 22).
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 4 through 9 (index after Section 3), corresponding to pages 6-22 of the source document.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.a_analysis: The primary issue is the omission of entire pages and sections (addressed in C3). Within the sections that are present (like the Eastern Region Report), the content is complete and faithful to the source.

checks.a_analysis: The text provided in the output segments matches the source PDF exactly, including minor idiosyncrasies like the typo 'work they do over.' in the Johnston County retirement paragraph on page 23. No meaningful text differences were found in the converted portions.

checks.a_analysis: The output document does not contain duplicated text blocks. Section 3 successfully consolidates the 'Eastern Region Report' from pages 3, 4, and 5 into a single structural unit without repeating the regional header or text across page boundaries. Other content, such as image captions and introductory text, correctly reflects the original layout where text is sometimes repeated between captions and main paragraphs.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. The document title is used as an H1, major section titles (like 'NEW 911 BOARD MEMBERS' and 'PSAP Highlights') are H2, and sub-reports or specific profile names (like 'The Eastern Region Report' and 'Brett Renfrow with Johnston County 911 Retires!') are H3. No heading levels are skipped.
The source document contains no tables, and the output correctly reports a table count of zero.

The reading order follows the logical flow of the newsletter. Elements like the date and edition are placed before the main article heading on page 1, and text is consistently placed before associated images, which is an acceptable accessibility optimization.

The document contains several lists (e.g., training classes on page 16, locations on page 18). While the relevant portions of the JSON are omitted in the snippet, there is no evidence of list corruption in the available sections, and simple paragraphs are used correctly where intended.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: There is a significant mismatch between the heading count in the JSON (31) and the rendered HTML (42). This difference of 11 headings exceeds the 10% threshold and the 3-heading tolerance, indicating that the renderer either added or failed to deduplicate headings (likely treating section titles as additional headings).
checks.b_evidence_source: Explicit counts provided: Headings in semantic.json: 31, Headings in rendered HTML: 42.
checks.b_evidence_output: Headings in semantic.json: 31, Headings in rendered HTML: 42.

checks.a_analysis: There is a significant mismatch between the number of links identified in the JSON (3) and those in the rendered HTML (35). The source document only contains a handful of actionable links (one URL, several emails, and a phone number). The HTML count suggests heavy link injection, likely auto-linking images or common text strings not intended as links.
checks.b_evidence_source: Explicit counts provided: Links in semantic.json: 3, Links in rendered HTML: 35.
checks.b_evidence_output: Links in semantic.json: 3, Links in rendered HTML: 35.
